response.justification: Sarah Smith is a seasoned full-stack and backend software developer with strong web, mobile, and low-latency systems experience, but her profile has no meaningful overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep hardware expertise — SoC and IP modeling, GPU architecture, AI accelerator optimization, and hardware-software co-design at the silicon level — none of which appear anywhere in her 14-year career. Her current AI-related work involves software agent frameworks, not deep-learning workload optimization or accelerator hardware, and her background in C/C++ is limited to a single IDE tooling role. The domain mismatch is fundamental, not bridgeable by seniority alone.
strengths: 14+ years of professional experience satisfies the BS+8yr minimum education/experience threshold
Demonstrated performance-conscious engineering in high-throughput systems (millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby)
Current role at re:cinq involves AI-adjacent infrastructure (MCP frameworks, agentic workflows), showing familiarity with the AI ecosystem
concerns: Zero SoC, IP modeling, or hardware architecture experience across the entire career
No GPU, SIMD/MIMD, or AI accelerator background — the core technical requirements of the role are entirely absent
Principal-level IC hardware architect role requires deep domain specialization; candidate's profile is that of a generalist web/backend developer

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware design experience
No AI accelerator or GPU architecture background
